title: Choking

steps.title: Check if they can cough
steps.instruction: If the person can cough forcefully, talk, or breathe — let them keep coughing. Do NOT intervene yet. Stay close and watch their condition carefully.

steps.title: Call for help
steps.instruction: If the person CANNOT cough, talk, cry or breathe — act immediately. Ask someone nearby to call emergency services (101) while you give first aid. If alone, call first then help.

steps.title: Give 5 back blows
steps.instruction: Stand to the side and slightly behind the person. Place one arm across their chest for support. Bend them forward at the waist. Strike firmly 5 times between the shoulder blades using the heel of your hand.

steps.title: Give 5 abdominal thrusts (Heimlich)
steps.instruction: Stand behind the person with one foot forward for balance. Make a fist and place it just above the navel. Grab your fist with your other hand. Press inward and upward with 5 quick, firm thrusts.

steps.title: Alternate and repeat
steps.instruction: Keep alternating between 5 back blows and 5 abdominal thrusts until the object is dislodged, the person can breathe or cough forcefully, or they become unconscious.

steps.title: If they become unconscious
steps.instruction: Lower the person carefully to the floor. Call 101 immediately if not already done. Look inside the mouth — if you can SEE the object, remove it with a finger sweep. NEVER do a blind finger sweep. Begin CPR immediately.

steps.title: Special cases
steps.instruction: If the person is pregnant or too large to wrap your arms around — use chest thrusts instead. Place your hands at the base of the breastbone and press hard inward with quick thrusts.

warnings: Do NOT do a blind finger sweep — only remove object if you can see it
Do NOT slap the back if the person can still cough forcefully
Do NOT hang a child upside down by their feet
Do NOT leave the person alone even if they seem to recover
sources: American Red Cross First Aid Guidelines
